preFix.title: Summer-and-winter weave on four shafts
preFix.subtitle: A reversible two-tie-unit structure where the pattern shows on both faces
preFix.excerpt: Summer-and-winter is a two-tie-unit weave structure in which pattern weft threads float over three warp ends at a time to build a figure that appears on both faces of the cloth, reversed in colour on the back. On four shafts, shafts 1 and 2 are the tie-down shafts and shafts 3 and 4 carry the pattern blocks.
preFix.sourceNotes: Threading and tie-up conventions from Hooper, Hand-Loom Weaving (1910), public domain, Internet Archive. The two-tie-unit principle is described in the chapter on supplementary-weft structures. Mary E. Black, The Key to Weaving (1945), cited as standard reference for summer-and-winter treadling sequence and reversibility; not quoted verbatim.

content.text: Summer-and-winter is named for the two-face reversibility of the cloth: the side that shows the light pattern (summer) has the same figure as the side showing the dark pattern (winter), but with the colours exchanged. This reversal happens because the
pattern shaft
threads lift to show the pattern weft on the face, while the non-lifted pattern threads show the background weft on the face in those same positions. The
tie-down shaft
threads interlock every three ends to keep the
floats
stable.
The draft

The threading alternates tie-down threads (shafts 1 and 2 alternating) with pattern threads (shaft 3 for block A, shaft 4 for block B). The standard unit is: 1, 3, 2, 3 for block A and 1, 4, 2, 4 for block B. Treadles 1 and 2 each lift one tie-down shaft with one pattern shaft (the pattern picks). Treadle 5 lifts tie-down shaft 1 alone and treadle 6 lifts tie-down shaft 2 alone (the ground picks). The treadling alternates pattern and ground picks throughout.
What you need

attrs.name: Four-shaft floor loom with at least six treadles
Boat shuttle for the pattern weft
Stick shuttle for the ground weft
Weaving reed, sett to suit yarn
Warp yarn: fine cotton or linen, pale neutral
Pattern weft: heavier yarn in a contrasting colour
Ground weft: same or finer yarn as warp

content.text: How the threading unit works

The four-thread unit (1, 3, 2, 3) places the tie-down threads on shafts 1 and 2 at positions 1 and 3 within the unit, and the pattern thread on shaft 3 (block A) at positions 2 and 4. This means the pattern weft floats over two pattern threads (positions 2 and 4) but is tied down between them at position 3 by a tie-down thread. The float length is three: from the last tie-down thread through two pattern threads to the next tie-down thread.

A float of three ends is long enough to produce a clear raised surface. It is also short enough to stay stable in furnishing or clothing cloth. Longer floats, such as overshot floats of five or seven ends, catch more easily in use. The summer-and-winter structure limits float length by construction, which is why it was popular for household weaving.
Treadling for the summer and winter sequence

The treadling alternates pattern picks (treadles 1-4, which lift one tie-down shaft plus one pattern shaft) and ground picks (treadles 5-6, which lift one tie-down shaft alone). Each pattern pick is followed by a ground pick before the next pattern pick. This two-pick sequence is the summer sequence when the pattern weft is lighter than the warp, and the winter sequence when the pattern weft is darker.
Treadle 1 (pattern block A, tie 1): throw pattern shuttle, beat.
Treadle 5 (ground tie 1): throw ground shuttle, beat.
Treadle 2 (pattern block A, tie 2): throw pattern shuttle, beat.
Treadle 5 (ground tie 1): throw ground shuttle, beat.
Repeat with treadles 3 and 4 for block B, using treadle 6 (ground tie 2) as the ground picks.
Why the cloth is identical on both faces

On the face of the cloth, the raised pattern threads show the pattern weft floating over them in the block areas. The ground weft sits between the tie-down threads in the non-block areas. On the back, the same blocks appear in the ground weft colour. The pattern weft is hidden under the pattern threads in those areas on the back. The ground weft, visible on the face in the non-block areas, is hidden on the back in those same positions. Both faces carry the same geometry. Only the colour assignment is exchanged.

This structure was used historically for reversible table runners and coverlets where both faces would be visible. Using a bold contrast between pattern and ground weft colours (navy and natural, or red and cream) makes the figure prominent on both faces.
Sett and beat

Summer-and-winter works well at a relaxed plain-weave sett: roughly the wraps-per-inch of the warp yarn divided by 2 for the sett in epi. At this sett the cloth is square when beaten to a balanced density. The pattern weft should be approximately twice the diameter of the ground weft so the float areas build up to the same height as the double-picked ground areas. If the pattern weft and ground weft are the same weight, beat the pattern picks more firmly.

attrs.fix: Check the tie-up. Treadle 1 should lift shafts 1 and 3 together. If it lifts only shaft 1, the tie-up is missing the pattern shaft connection. Re-tie each pattern treadle to include the correct shaft pair.
attrs.cause: The treadle tie-up is connecting each treadle to only one shaft rather than one tie-down plus one pattern shaft
attrs.problem: The pattern does not appear; the cloth looks like two-colour plain weave

attrs.fix: Check the threading sequence in the problem area. Every pattern thread should be flanked by tie-down threads. Re-thread any pair where two adjacent ends are both on shaft 3 or both on shaft 4.
attrs.cause: Threading error: two adjacent threads have both been placed on pattern shafts rather than alternating tie-down and pattern
attrs.problem: The floats are longer than three ends in some areas

attrs.fix: Use clearly contrasting colours for pattern and ground weft. A mid-value ground weft and a dark pattern weft will give insufficient contrast; aim for at least two to three values of difference on the light-to-dark scale.
attrs.cause: The ground weft is the same colour as the pattern weft, or too similar to read as a contrast
attrs.problem: The summer and winter faces look the same colour rather than reversed
